title: festuca viridula var. howellii
intro: Festuca viridula var. howellii, also known as Howell's fescue, is a rare and endangered variety of grass native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits associated with Festuca viridula var. howellii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
